body.title: [triage] Bulk migrate goals, decisions, slates, sessions to filesystem (projection mode)
body.description:
**Problem:** Old records (decisions before D-filesystem-memory-store-2026-06-16, goals, slates, sessions) still live in DB palace only. Full picture of "decisions → tickets → commits" can't be built until all records exist in devlab/runtime/memory/.

**Affected files:**
- devlab/claudecode/migrate_decisions.py (exists, used for 36 decisions pilot)
- devlab/claudecode/migrate_goals.py (new)
- devlab/claudecode/migrate_slates.py (new, or integrated)
- devlab/claudecode/migrate_sessions.py (new, or integrated)

**Scope boundary:** Idempotent projection of all existing DB palace records into `devlab/runtime/memory/` JSON files with original timestamps. No deletions. Source stays authoritative (dual-write continues). If a record already exists in filesystem (same semantic id), atomic overwrite with same timestamp (no new file).

**Completion criteria:**
1. All goals from DB palace → goals/*.json in filesystem (retain original created_at timestamp)
2. All decisions from DB palace → decisions/*.json (pilot migrated 36/36; this completes the backlog)
3. All slates from DB palace → slates/*.json (or decline as non-canonical, document reason)
4. All sessions → sessions/*.json (or decline, document reason)
5. Run migrate scripts on fresh DB copy; verify idempotence (second run = zero new files)
6. Grep for all semantic-ids in devlab/runtime/memory/ — should have every goal/decision from DB

**Acceptance test:** 
```bash
psql "$UU_HOME_DB_URL" -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_palace WHERE path LIKE 'D-%'" \
  | grep -oE '[0-9]+' > /tmp/db_count.txt
find devlab/runtime/memory/decisions -name '*.json' | wc -l > /tmp/fs_count.txt
# Both counts should match within rounding error (DB might have dupes)
```

**Design rules:** Projection not relocation; idempotent via original timestamp; semantic ids only; chokepoint=memory_emit.py

**Test plan:** Haiku migration script (low-cost); verify counts match; spot-check 10 random records (compare DB vs filesystem JSON).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
